metadata.name: Paper Mandala #354
metadata.description:
A collaboration with P1xelboy and Paper Buddha.
Beautiful paper mandalas made purely with code in Paper Buddha's style.
Features include:
27 different palettes
3 different mandala types (Lotus, Starburst Style 1 and Starburst Style 2)
Four sections of the mandala with one of 10 different pattern types (Dots, Checks, Crosses, Triangles, Stripes, Circles, Polygons, Lazers, Quadrants and Hatching)
4 different direction types of shadow - of varying length
Randomly placed star background
Randomly placed Nebula
Possible Special Pattern Effect with darker stippling pattern
Possible Nirvana Effect where random colors are added to one of either Golden, Silver, Bronze, Blue Moon or Sea Green palettes.

Using only code, this produces a unique output which does take a few seconds to load.
Press "S" to Save your unique Paper Mandala as a 2048 x 2048 JPG
Press "R" for a random shuffle effect - (please wait for reload) - Refresh page to reset

The project works in a responsive browser, so to set up for phone background or watch, resize the browser accordingly and Alt + Print Screen to get an appropriately sized image.

Offered as a Dutch Auction, holders of the first 20 tokens (1-20) will receive additional benefits in the form of utility for future releases in the P1xelboy/Paper Mandala collaboration series. (exact details to be determined)
This utility will remain attached to tokens 1-20 and transfer to the new owner upon subsequent secondary sales.

Uses p5.js and p5.pattern
@p1x3lboy @paperbuddha - September 2022

metadata.name: SCREENSAVER #89
metadata.description:
DIYFRAME is a decentralized web app by Andre Fuchs that lets you display fx(hash), objkt, versum and teia NFTs on any screen for free.

+ Sign up now at https://diyframe.xyz
__
NFT by Andre Fuchs for DIYFRAME
2023, https://andrefuchs.xyz
__
All proceeds will be used to fund the development of DIYFRAME.

metadata.name: Possible Ways #103
metadata.description:
This project is written in javascript without using any other libraries, and it is an endless real-time software animation.

The appearance of each token is initially determined by the hash, but the size and positions of its elements change randomly over time, creating an infinite performance.

While working on this project, I was inspired by the elusiveness of the moment, that cannot be saved or predicted. It can only be experienced.

metadata.name: RULES (for Elementary Cellular Automata) #197
metadata.description:
An important element of generative art is emergence. The most famous algorithm for this is probably Cellular Automata (CA).

However, numerous generative artists have created works using CA, which has lost its visual freshness and is like chewing gum that has lost its taste.

Although it is such a classic algorithm, adding my own little ideas to it and finding a new "flavor" is the reason why I code.

Here, the subject is 1D Elementary Cellular Automata, the simplest of all cellular automata. 1D Elementary Cellular Automata has 256 different rules, which change the shapes produced. This work aims to create more complex shapes by randomly selecting some of those rules and placing multiple of them on the screen.

The on/off of the CA cell is often likened to the behavior of living creatures.As of 2022, in the country where I live, prices are rising, wages are not increasing, and COVID-19 has not yet ended. I created this work with the image of people at the mercy of these various rules.

---

Screen click: Change rules
S key: Save png
L key: Show/hide grid

metadata.name: Quantum ATLAS #5
metadata.description:
Quantum ATLAS

This artwork was inspired by high-energy physics — the branch of physics that deals with the study of the fundamental particles and forces that make up the universe. It seeks to understand the structure and behavior of matter at the smallest scales, exploring the most fundamental building blocks of nature and the interactions between them.

High-energy physics investigates fundamental particles, such as quarks, leptons, and bosons, which are the building blocks of atoms and matter as we know it. It aims to answer questions about their properties, masses, charges, and the forces that govern their interactions. This field of study relies on powerful particle accelerators, such as the Large Hadron Collider, to create high-energy collisions between particles and observe the resulting reactions.

Detectors play a crucial role in high-energy physics experiments. They are sophisticated instruments designed to detect, measure, and analyze the particles and their properties that are produced during high-energy collisions in particle accelerators. Detectors enable scientists to collect data and gain insights into the fundamental particles and forces that govern the universe

The ATLAS detector is a large particle detector, one of the four main detectors at the Large Hadron Collider. ATLAS stands for "A Toroidal LHC ApparatuS" and is designed to investigate various aspects of particle physics, including the search for new particles, the study of the Higgs boson, and the exploration of fundamental forces and particles. The detector is massive in size, measuring about 46 meters in length and 25 meters in height.

By analyzing the vast amount of data produced by the collisions, scientists can gain insights into the fundamental nature of matter, unravel the mysteries of the universe, and potentially make groundbreaking discoveries that contribute to our understanding of the fundamental laws of physics.

Quantum ATLAS is my artistic endeavor that seeks to emulate the infinite variety of forms that elementary particles can exhibit during collisions within detectors. From elegant spirals and swirling vortexes to branching networks and pulsating clusters, it unveils a visual tapestry of shapes and structures that echo the fundamental processes occurring at the quantum level. The intricate lines in the artwork simulate the paths of particles as they traverse the detector, leaving behind traces of their presence.

Please use fullscreen or download artwork in high resolution for a better viewing experience. Chrome is highly recommended for high res saving.

Made with love, math, physics, and p5js.


Controls in fullscreen:
[1] - save PNG  1880 x 1280
[2] - save PNG 3760 x 2560
[3] - save PNG 7520 x 5120
[4] - save PNG 15040 x 10240

On mobile:
[quick tap] - save PNG

metadata.name: glory hole$ #283
metadata.description:
our consciousness as humans can be distilled into a crudely fashioned hole at an abandoned dave & busters and the meditative state that is endured while sitting on a neglected toilet praying for the sound of disrupted waters. glory holes embody anonymity and pleasure for pleasure's sake. they are creations that cannot be destroyed, as they exist in the memories of anyone who uses or witnesses. many speculate that the carving of the hole is the purest form of human ingenuity and the desire to explore the unknown. formerly limited to the physical space. these glory holes transcend space and time. and exist in that same unknown that tempts us all. legalize ground beef. create nothing. consume everything.

HUGE thank you to @PureSpider for the layered PNG template, this dream would not have been realized without you friend.

metadata.name: Lines, just lines #11
metadata.description:
"Lines, just lines" is my reflection on the beauty of simplicity and perpetual motion. At first glance, they are just lines dancing on a digital canvas. Yet, their complex and ever-changing choreography reveals an unexpected depth. I have created a ballet of lines that move with fluid grace, influenced by invisible forces such as "attractors" and "repulsors." These elements introduce an unpredictable dynamic, creating hypnotic movements that mirror the ordered chaos of nature.

The apparent simplicity of the work is deceptive, hiding a complex universe that translates this chaos into hypnotic movements. This piece invites viewers to lose themselves in a constant flow, to meditate on the emerging patterns and ephemeral forms that appear and disappear. It is a celebration of minimalism, where repetition and subtle variation create a captivating visual experience.

In our world saturated with visual stimuli, "Lines, just lines" offers a moment of pause, an opportunity to contemplate pure movement and form in its raw state. It reminds us that sometimes, lines, just lines, can tell an infinite and fascinating story.
